```{r}
      tert_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m2 <- coxph(f_tert_m2, data = dat)

  res_overall_cox_imaging[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M2_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m2,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model2_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2, term_T3)
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 3
  f_tert_m3 <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s*Sex + Age + %s +
       BC_PC1 + BC_PC2 + BC_PC3 + BC_PC4 + BC_PC5 + TTFFMVi",
      tert_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m3 <- coxph(f_tert_m3, data = dat)

  res_overall_cox_imaging[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M3_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m3,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model3_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2, term_T3)
    )
}

res_overall_cox_imaging <- bind_rows(res_overall_cox_imaging)

##-------------------------------
## 1B. Sex-stratified
##-------------------------------
res_sex_cox_imaging <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(sex_specs_imaging))) {
  sp_name <- sex_specs_imaging$exposure_type[i]
  sex_i   <- sex_specs_imaging$sex[i]

  if (sp_name == "PC2") {
    dat_all <- Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df
    cont_x  <- "rt_PC2"
    bmd_x   <- "Total_BMD"
    tert_M  <- "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident"
    tert_F  <- "PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident"
  } else {
    dat_all <- Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df
    cont_x  <- "rt_BMFF"
    bmd_x   <- "Spine_BMD"
    tert_M  <- "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident"
    tert_F  <- "Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident"
  }

  dat <- dat_all %>% dplyr::filter(Sex == sex_i)
  tert_x_sex <- if (sex_i == "M") tert_M else tert_F

  # Continuous – Model 1
  f_cont_m1_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI",
      cont_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m1_sex <- coxph(f_cont_m1_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_imaging[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M1_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m1_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model1_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Continuous – Model 2
  f_cont_m2_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI + %s",
      cont_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m2_sex <- coxph(f_cont_m2_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_imaging[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M2_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m2_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model2_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Continuous – Model 3
  f_cont_m3_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + %s +
       BC_PC1 + BC_PC2 + BC_PC3 + BC_PC4 + BC_PC5 + TTFFMVi",
      cont_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m3_sex <- coxph(f_cont_m3_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_imaging[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M3_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m3_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model3_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 1
  f_tert_m1_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI",
      tert_x_sex
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m1_sex <- coxph(f_tert_m1_sex, data = dat)
  term_T2_sex <- paste0(tert_x_sex, "T2")
  term_T3_sex <- paste0(tert_x_sex, "T3")

  res_sex_cox_imaging[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M1_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m1_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model1_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2_sex, term_T3_sex)
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 2
  f_tert_m2_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI + %s",
      tert_x_sex, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m2_sex <- coxph(f_tert_m2_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_imaging[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M2_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m2_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model2_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2_sex, term_T3_sex)
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 3
  f_tert_m3_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + %s +
       BC_PC1 + BC_PC2 + BC_PC3 + BC_PC4 + BC_PC5 + TTFFMVi",
      tert_x_sex, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m3_sex <- coxph(f_tert_m3_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_imaging[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M3_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m3_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "imaging",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model3_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2_sex, term_T3_sex)
    )
}

res_sex_cox_imaging <- bind_rows(res_sex_cox_imaging)

cox_results_imaging <- bind_rows(res_overall_cox_imaging, res_sex_cox_imaging)

#==========================================================
# 2. ADMISSION-INCIDENT ANALYSES (same models, new vars)
#==========================================================

##-------------------------------
## 2A. Overall
##-------------------------------
res_overall_cox_admission <- list()

for (sp in overall_specs_admission) {
  dat      <- sp$df
  exposure <- sp$exposure_type
  cont_x   <- sp$cont_var
  tert_x   <- sp$tert_var
  bmd_x    <- sp$bmd_var

  # Continuous – Model 1
  f_cont_m1 <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s*Sex + Age + BMI",
      cont_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m1 <- coxph(f_cont_m1, data = dat)

  res_overall_cox_admission[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M1_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m1,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model1_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Continuous – Model 2
  f_cont_m2 <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s*Sex + Age + BMI + %s",
      cont_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m2 <- coxph(f_cont_m2, data = dat)

  res_overall_cox_admission[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M2_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m2,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model2_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Continuous – Model 3
  f_cont_m3 <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s*Sex + Age + %s +
       BC_PC1 + BC_PC2 + BC_PC3 + BC_PC4 + BC_PC5 + TTFFMVi",
      cont_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m3 <- coxph(f_cont_m3, data = dat)

  res_overall_cox_admission[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M3_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m3,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model3_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 1
  f_tert_m1 <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s*Sex + Age + BMI",
      tert_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m1 <- coxph(f_tert_m1, data = dat)
  term_T2 <- paste0(tert_x, "T2")
  term_T3 <- paste0(tert_x, "T3")

  res_overall_cox_admission[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M1_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m1,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model1_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2, term_T3)
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 2
  f_tert_m2 <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s*Sex + Age + BMI + %s",
      tert_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m2 <- coxph(f_tert_m2, data = dat)

  res_overall_cox_admission[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M2_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m2,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model2_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2, term_T3)
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 3
  f_tert_m3 <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s*Sex + Age + %s +
       BC_PC1 + BC_PC2 + BC_PC3 + BC_PC4 + BC_PC5 + TTFFMVi",
      tert_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m3 <- coxph(f_tert_m3, data = dat)

  res_overall_cox_admission[[paste0("overall_", exposure, "_M3_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m3,
      analysis_id   = paste0("overall_", exposure),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = exposure,
      sex_stratum   = "All",
      model_label   = "Model3_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2, term_T3)
    )
}

res_overall_cox_admission <- bind_rows(res_overall_cox_admission)

##-------------------------------
## 2B. Sex-stratified
##-------------------------------
res_sex_cox_admission <- list()

for (i in seq_len(nrow(sex_specs_admission))) {
  sp_name <- sex_specs_admission$exposure_type[i]
  sex_i   <- sex_specs_admission$sex[i]

  if (sp_name == "PC2") {
    dat_all <- Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df
    cont_x  <- "rt_PC2"
    bmd_x   <- "Total_BMD"
    tert_M  <- "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident"
    tert_F  <- "PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"
  } else {
    dat_all <- Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df
    cont_x  <- "rt_BMFF"
    bmd_x   <- "Spine_BMD"
    tert_M  <- "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident"
    tert_F  <- "Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"
  }

  dat <- dat_all %>% dplyr::filter(Sex == sex_i)
  tert_x_sex <- if (sex_i == "M") tert_M else tert_F

  # Continuous – Model 1
  f_cont_m1_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI",
      cont_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m1_sex <- coxph(f_cont_m1_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_admission[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M1_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m1_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model1_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Continuous – Model 2
  f_cont_m2_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI + %s",
      cont_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m2_sex <- coxph(f_cont_m2_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_admission[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M2_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m2_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model2_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Continuous – Model 3
  f_cont_m3_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + %s +
       BC_PC1 + BC_PC2 + BC_PC3 + BC_PC4 + BC_PC5 + TTFFMVi",
      cont_x, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_cont_m3_sex <- coxph(f_cont_m3_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_admission[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M3_cont")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_cont_m3_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model3_cont",
      terms         = cont_x
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 1
  f_tert_m1_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI",
      tert_x_sex
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m1_sex <- coxph(f_tert_m1_sex, data = dat)
  term_T2_sex <- paste0(tert_x_sex, "T2")
  term_T3_sex <- paste0(tert_x_sex, "T3")

  res_sex_cox_admission[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M1_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m1_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model1_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2_sex, term_T3_sex)
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 2
  f_tert_m2_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + BMI + %s",
      tert_x_sex, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m2_sex <- coxph(f_tert_m2_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_admission[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M2_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m2_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model2_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2_sex, term_T3_sex)
    )

  # Tertiles – Model 3
  f_tert_m3_sex <- as.formula(
    sprintf(
      "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ %s + Age + %s +
       BC_PC1 + BC_PC2 + BC_PC3 + BC_PC4 + BC_PC5 + TTFFMVi",
      tert_x_sex, bmd_x
    )
  )
  fit_tert_m3_sex <- coxph(f_tert_m3_sex, data = dat)

  res_sex_cox_admission[[paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i, "_M3_tert")]] <-
    extract_terms(
      fit_tert_m3_sex,
      analysis_id   = paste0("sex_", sp_name, "_", sex_i),
      outcome_type  = "admission",
      exposure_type = sp_name,
      sex_stratum   = sex_i,
      model_label   = "Model3_tertiles",
      terms         = c(term_T2_sex, term_T3_sex)
    )
}

res_sex_cox_admission <- bind_rows(res_sex_cox_admission)

cox_results_admission <- bind_rows(res_overall_cox_admission, res_sex_cox_admission)

#==========================================================
# FINAL: separate result data frames
#==========================================================
# Imaging-incident Cox results (Models 1–3, PC2/Spine, overall & sex-stratified)
cox_results_imaging

# Admission-incident Cox results (Models 1–3, PC2/Spine, overall & sex-stratified)
cox_results_admission

```


Present the PC2 and Spine BMFF tertile and normalised exposures forest plot
```{r}
#Imaging incident
mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_PC2*Sex+Age+Total_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~PC2_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident*Sex+Age+Total_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit
summary(mv_fit)

#Admission incident
mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_PC2*Sex+Age+Total_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)~PC2_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident*Sex+Age+Total_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit
summary(mv_fit)$coefficients
summary(mv_fit)

#Imaging incident
mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_BMFF*Sex+Age+Spine_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~Spine_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident*Sex+Age+Spine_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit
summary(mv_fit)

#Admission incident
mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_BMFF*Sex+Age+Spine_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)~Spine_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident*Sex+Age+Spine_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df)

mv_fit
summary(mv_fit)

#Plot

#Imaging incident
Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df <- data.frame(
  Index = c(1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8), 
  Predictor = c("Spine BMFF", "Spine BMFF", "Spine BMFF", "Spine BMFF", "PC2", "PC2", "PC2", "PC2"),
  Exposure = c("Spine T1 (ref)", "Spine T2", "Spine T3", "Spine Rank-normalized", "PC2 T1 (ref)", "PC2 T2", "PC2 T3", "PC2 Rank-normalized"),
  Exposure_type = c("T1", "T2", "T3", "normalized", "T1", "T2", "T3", "normalized"),
  OR = c(1.000, 1.631, 2.298, 1.229, 1.000, 1.834, 3.244, 1.414),
  LL = c(1.000, 1.053, 1.371, 1.048, 1.000, 0.955, 1.642, 1.190),
  UL = c(1.000, 2.526, 3.853, 1.427, 1.000, 3.524, 6.408, 1.681),
  p = c(0.075, 0.028, 1.6e-3, 0.011, 0.075, 0.069, 7e-4, 8.53e-5)
)

#Admission incident
Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df <- data.frame(
  Index = c(1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8), 
  Predictor = c("Spine BMFF", "Spine BMFF", "Spine BMFF", "Spine BMFF", "PC2", "PC2", "PC2", "PC2"),
  Exposure = c("Spine T1 (ref)", "Spine T2", "Spine T3", "Spine Rank-normalized", "PC2 T1 (ref)", "PC2 T2", "PC2 T3", "PC2 Rank-normalized"),
  Exposure_type = c("T1", "T2", "T3", "normalized", "T1", "T2", "T3", "normalized"),
  OR = c(1.000, 1.240, 2.061, 1.367, 1.000, 1.793, 3.857, 1.814),
  LL = c(1.000, 1.032, 1.674, 1.254, 1.000, 1.392, 2.972, 1.651),
  UL = c(1.000, 1.490, 2.537, 1.490, 1.000, 2.309, 5.006, 1.992),
  p = c(0.075, 0.022, 9.47e-12, 1.29e-12, 0.075, 6.08e-06, 3.40e-24, 1.84e-35)
)


Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df$Exposure_type <- factor(
  Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df$Exposure_type,
  levels = c("T1", "T2", "T3", "normalized")
)

ggplot(Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df, aes(y = Index, x = OR, col = Exposure)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = -log10(p), shape=Exposure_type)) +  
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = LL, xmax = UL), height = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color = "darkred", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  xlab("Hazard Ratio (95% CI)") + 
  ylab(" ") + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.text  = element_text(face = "bold")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spine T1 (ref)" = "steelblue", "Spine T2" = "steelblue", "Spine T3" = "steelblue", "Spine Rank-normalized" = "steelblue", "PC2 T1 (ref)" = "darkorange", "PC2 T2" = "darkorange", "PC2 T3" = "darkorange", "PC2 Rank-normalized" = "darkorange"),
                     labels = c("Spine T1 (ref)" = "Spine BMFF T1 (ref)", "Spine T2" = "Spine BMFF T2", "Spine T3" = "Spine BMFF T3", "Spine Rank-normalized" = "Spine BMFF (normalised)", "PC2 (ref)" = "PC2 T1 (ref)", "PC2 T2" = "PC2 T2", "PC2 T3" = "PC2 T3", "PC2 Rank-normalized" = "PC2 (normalised)"))+
  scale_y_reverse()+
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("T1" = 15, "T2" = 16, "T3" = 17, "normalized" = 18))

#Other way of presenting it - use this for manuscript
Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df = Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df[c(1,5,2,6,3,7,4,8),]
Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df$Index = seq_along(Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df$Index)

#Imaging-incident
p = ggplot(Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df, aes(y = Index, x = OR, col = Predictor)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = 7)) +  
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = LL, xmax = UL), height = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color = "darkred", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  xlab("Hazard Ratio (95% CI)") + 
  ylab(" ") + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.text  = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spine BMFF" = "steelblue", "PC2" = "darkorange"),
                     labels = c("Spine BMFF" = "Spine BMFF", "PC2" = "PC2"))+
  scale_y_reverse()+
  guides(shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank())+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2.5, color = "lightgray", linetype = "dashed", cex = 0.5)+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4.5, color = "lightgray", linetype = "dashed", cex = 0.5)+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 6.5, color = "lightgray", linetype = "dashed", cex = 0.5)+
  xlim(-1,6.5)+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=1, label="Spine BMFF T1\n (ref)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=2, label="PC2 T1\n (ref)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=3, label="Spine BMFF T2\n HR = 1.631 (1.053, 2.526)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=4, label="PC2 T2\n HR = 1.834 (0.955, 3.524)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=5, label="Spine BMFF T3\n HR = 2.298 (1.371, 3.853)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=6, label="PC2 T3\n HR = 3.244 (1.642, 6.408)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=7, label="Spine BMFF (normalized)\n HR = 1.229 (1.048, 1.427)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=8, label="PC2 T2 (normalized)\nHR = 1.414 (1.190, 1.681)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  theme(axis.title.y=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank())

p
  
ggsave(
  filename = "Cox_imaging_incident_T2D.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 20,
  height = 12
)

#Admission incident
p = ggplot(Spine_BMFF_PC2_PRSCS_A_B_C_D_T2D_df, aes(y = Index, x = OR, col = Predictor)) +
  geom_point(aes(size=7)) +  
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = LL, xmax = UL), height = 0) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, color = "darkred", linetype = "dashed", size = 0.5, alpha = 0.5) +
  xlab("Hazard Ratio (95% CI)") + 
  ylab(" ") + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 25, face = "bold")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.title = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold")) +
  theme(legend.text  = element_text(size = 25,face = "bold")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spine BMFF" = "steelblue", "PC2" = "darkorange"),
                     labels = c("Spine BMFF" = "Spine BMFF", "PC2" = "PC2"))+
  scale_y_reverse()+
  guides(shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5))) +
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(), panel.grid.minor = element_blank())+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2.5, color = "lightgray", linetype = "dashed", cex = 0.5)+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 4.5, color = "lightgray", linetype = "dashed", cex = 0.5)+
  geom_hline(yintercept = 6.5, color = "lightgray", linetype = "dashed", cex = 0.5)+
  xlim(-1,6.5)+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=1, label="Spine BMFF T1\n (ref)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=2, label="PC2 T1\n (ref)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=3, label="Spine BMFF T2\n HR = 1.240 (1.032, 1.490)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=4, label="PC2 T2\n HR = 1.793 (1.392, 2.309)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=5, label="Spine BMFF T3\n HR = 2.061 (1.674, 2.537)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=6, label="PC2 T3\n HR = 3.857 (2.972, 5.006)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=7, label="Spine BMFF (normalized)\n HR = 1.367 (1.254, 1.490)", color="steelblue", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  annotate(geom="text", x=-0.25, y=8, label="PC2 T2 (normalized)\nHR = 1.814 (1.651, 1.992)", color="darkorange", size = 9, fontface = "bold")+
  theme(axis.title.y=element_blank(),
        axis.text.y=element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.y=element_blank())

p

ggsave(
  filename = "Cox_admission_incident_T2D.png",
  plot = p,
  width = 20,
  height = 12
)
```


Cross-validate the fully adjusted (model 3) imag/adm inc.-T2D~PC2/spine BMFF regressions for improved internal validity - mention that since we currently have not replicated these findings using the gold-standard approach of testing the models in other cohorts, we applied cross-validation techniques to improve the internal validity of the results.
- K-fold CV the effect estimates, AUC (as done below) and RCS (if possible) model 3 - adjusted Cox models 
- Really good peanalised Cox regression work pipeline - including model calibration: https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/hdnom/vignettes/hdnom.html#Model_calibration 
```{r}
#Spine BMFF - both sexes and in males and females separately

#Model performance evaluation - - use type = "risk" or "lp" for the predict() function of coxph to get the predicted risk score (relative hazard at time t - in this case the risk of ever developing T2D during the whole follow‑up) for the outcome --> The current AUC = “How well do the Cox risk scores separate people who ever get T2D during follow‑up from those who never do?”

#AUC when training the model on a training data subset and testing it on a testing subset = 0.7665 (0.7227, 0.8102)

#k-Fold Cross-Validation approach --> more robust for improved internal validity (https://www.bmj.com/content/386/bmj-2023-078276 ; https://www.bmj.com/content/384/bmj-2023-074819) - do not split into training and test data sets as I am not tuning any model hyperparameters as you would for model selection using penalised regression (e.g., LASSO, Ridge, elastic net), where it is more appropriate to tune the hyperparameters in a separate data set to the one where you assess the model performance (such as nested cross-validation). Here I am running the model on the whole data set and then calculating the AUC using the same model parameters and then running the k-fold cross-validation to obtain the optimism corrected AUCs (which are lower than the AUCs calculated from the test data set from above).  

#Reasons for why I can't use the k-fold CV-ed linear predictors in the predict() function for the test-data subset:
#The cross-validated lp are an ensemble of fold-specific predictions only for training data, not for any new/unseen data.
#To compute a meaningful AUC on the test set using lp, you would need a single scoring function that maps test-set covariates to a risk score. In CV, each observation in the training data has an lp from a fold-specific model where that observation was in the test set. There’s no one model trained on the full training data (or on a predefined subset) whose lp you can apply to the test set unless you refit on the training data or construct an ensemble in a principled way (e.g., average lp from fold-models trained on training folds and then apply to the test data). Without that, you’re effectively using inconsistent scores.
#If you naively mix CV lp with test-set outcomes, you risk optimistic or biased performance estimates. The CV process is designed to provide an unbiased estimate of out-of-sample performance for the training procedure, but once you apply those CV-derived scores to a separate test set, you break the intended interpretation of the CV estimate.

#Create a for-loop from the code below to calculate the AUC for all exposures, models and sexes (next code chunk)

#Please generate a for-loop from the code below and the instructions that carries out the k-fold cross validated AUC (95% CI) calculations using spine BMFF and PC2 (using rt_BMFF and rt_PC2 as the continuous exposures) and the following tertiles for admission incident and imaging incident T2D in both sexes and in males (Sex==M) and females (Sex==F) separately. Tertiles to use in the model 1, 2, 3 regressions:

#PC2: "PC2_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident", "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident", "PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident", "PC2_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident", "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident","PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"

#Spine: "Spine_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident", "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident", "Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident", "Spine_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident", "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident","Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"

#Continuous exposure regressions:
#Model 1 = rt_BMFF or rt_PC2 * Sex + Age + BMI
#Model 2 = rt_BMFF or rt_PC2 * Sex + Age + BMI + Spine_BMD (for rt_BMFF) or + Total_BMD (for rt_PC2)
#Model 3 = rt_BMFF or rt_PC2 * Sex + Age + Spine_BMD (for rt_BMFF) or + Total_BMD (for rt_PC2) + BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi

#Repeat the continuous exposure regressions but using each of the tertiles listed above (where the spine tertiles and PC2 tertiles replace rt_BMFF and rt_PC2, respectively)

#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#Spine BMFF - both sexes and in males and females separately
#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(caret)
library(survival)
library(pROC)

set.seed(123)

#Change based on imaging or admission incident data as the prevalent T2D numbers differ
Spine_T2D_cox_df_model1 = na.omit(Spine_T2D_cox_RCS_df[,c(1,2,5:15)]) #imaging incident
Spine_T2D_cox_df_model1 = na.omit(Spine_T2D_cox_RCS_df[,c(3:15)]) #admission incident

train_data <- Spine_T2D_cox_df_model1 #Spine BMFF data frame: use Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df for PC2 (rt_PC2 as the primary exposure variable)

k <- 10
folds <- createFolds(train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox, k = k, list = TRUE) #Create folds for Imaging_incident_T2D_cox variable when running cox models with T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox below

# one out-of-sample prediction per training subject
cv_risk_train <- numeric(nrow(train_data))

for (i in seq_len(k)) {
  # fold i is the TEST portion within training
  test_indices_fold  <- folds[[i]]
  train_indices_fold <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(train_data)), test_indices_fold)
  
  train_fold <- train_data[train_indices_fold, ]
  test_fold  <- train_data[test_indices_fold, ]
  
  # Fit Cox model on this fold's training data: use Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) for imaging incident and rt_PC2 when using the Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df and run the regressions in both sexes (as shown below) and separately in males (subset to Sex==M) and females (subset to Sex==F) and remove Sex as an interactive covariate for the sex-stratified regressions
  sfit <- coxph(
    Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ rt_BMFF*Sex+Age+Spine_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi,
    data = train_fold
  )
  
  # Predict risk on the held-out fold
  cv_risk_train[test_indices_fold] <- predict(
    sfit,
    newdata = test_fold,
    type = "lp"
  )
}

# Cross-validated ROC/AUC: change to Imaging_incident_T2D_cox for imaging-incident regressions
roc_cv_train_spine_model_3 <- roc(
  response  = train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox,
  predictor = cv_risk_train,
  quiet     = TRUE
)

#Extract the AUC and CI (95% CI) and store in a data frame alongside all of the other regressions specified above
auc_cv_train <- auc(roc_cv_train_spine_model_3)
auc_cv_train
ci_cv_train  <- ci.auc(roc_cv_train_spine_model_3)
ci_cv_train

#Optimism-corrected AUC in the whole data set - use this as the final predictive performance metric, alongside calibration plot etc... 

#Model 3 (imaging-incident) = 0.764 (0.745 0.783)
#Model 3 (admission-incident) = 0.787 (0.777, 0.798)

#Out-of-sample C-index and calibration plots

# Add cross-validated linear predictor to data
train_data$cv_lp <- cv_risk_train

# Compute Harrell's C-index/statistic (chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/survival/vignettes/concordance.pdf) using cv_lp as the predictor
c_index_cv <- survConcordance(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ cv_lp,
  data = train_data
)

# C-index
c_index_cv$concordance
# 95% CI (approx)
c_index_cv$concordance + c(-1, 1) * 1.96 * c_index_cv$std.err

#C-index (admission-incident) = 0.7830895 (0.7694902, 0.7966887)
#C-index (imaging-incident) = 0.7724222 (0.7467651, 0.7980794)

#Calibration plot
library(survival)
library(rms)

# Check outcome is numeric 0/1
train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox_Score <- ifelse(train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox == 2, 1, 0) #re-code to 1 and 0

# Fit the Cox model with the cross-validated linear predictor
sfit <- coxph(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox_Score) ~ cv_lp,
  data = train_data,
  x = TRUE, y = TRUE
)

# Choose a time horizon for calibration (e.g. 90th percentile of follow-up)
time_horizon = 2970 #The day after the last T2D admission incidence: 5601 for admission, 2970 for imaging

# Predicted survival at time_horizon
sf <- survfit(sfit, newdata = train_data)

# Extract S(t) for each subject at time_horizon
# Build a matrix of survival probs: rows = times, cols = subjects
S_mat <- summary(sf, times = time_horizon)$surv
# For a `coxph` with individual newdata, `summary(sf, times=...)$surv` is in subject order
# Predicted risk = 1 - survival
p_hat <- 1 - S_mat   # vector of predicted event probabilities at time_horizon

# Observed outcome (0/1)
y <- train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox_Score

#Use 'val.prob.ci.2' function from: https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/CalibrationCurves/versions/2.0.7/topics/val.prob.ci.2
library(CalibrationCurves)
val.prob.ci.2(p_hat, y, CL.smooth="fill", smooth='rcs', nr.knots = 5, col.smooth = 'darkred', lwd.smooth = 2, logistic.cal=TRUE, col.log = 'steelblue', lwd.log = 2, dostats = FALSE, cl.level = FALSE, roundstats = 3, xlab = "Predicted probability", ylab = "Observed probability", pl=TRUE, legendloc = c(.2, .9) )

#predictABLE plot for grouped calibration plot
train_data = as.data.frame(train_data)
plotCalibration(data = train_data, cOutcome = 15, predRisk = p_hat, groups = 1000, xlabel = "Predicted probability", ylabel = "Observed probability")

#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#PC2 - both sexes and in males and females separately
#----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#Change based on imaging or admission incident data as the prevalent T2D numbers differ
PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1 = na.omit(PC2_T2D_cox_RCS_df[,c(1,2,5:15)])
PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1 = na.omit(PC2_T2D_cox_RCS_df[,c(3:15)])

mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_PC2*Sex+Age+Total_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi, data=PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1)
mv_fit
summary(mv_fit)
PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1$cox_beta = predict(mv_fit, newdata = PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1, type = "lp")

roc_obj  <- roc(PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox, PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1$cox_beta, quiet = TRUE)
auc_val  <- auc(roc_obj)
auc_val
ci_auc   <- ci.auc(roc_obj)
ci_auc

#Summary stats = 1.4139 (1.1895, 1.6805), p=8.53e-05 
#AUC when using the exact same data to train and test the model on = 0.7765 (0.7566, 0.7965)

#Create an 80/20 train–test split (stratified on the outcome) - not cross-validated
train_index <- createDataPartition(
  PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox,  # or use Incident_T2D_cox if preferred
  p = 0.8,
  list = FALSE
)

train_data <- PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1[train_index, ]
test_data  <- PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1[-train_index, ]

#Fit the Cox model on the training data
sfit <- coxph(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_PC2*Sex+Age+Total_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi,
  data = train_data
)

#Predict risk scores on the test data
test_data$surv_prob <- predict(sfit, newdata = test_data, type = "lp")

#Compute ROC and AUC on the test set
roc_obj  <- roc(test_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox, test_data$surv_prob, quiet = TRUE)
auc_val  <- auc(roc_obj)
auc_val
ci_auc   <- ci.auc(roc_obj)
ci_auc

#AUC when training the model on a training data subset and testing it on a testing subset = 0.7665 (0.7227, 0.8102)

#k-Fold Cross-Validation approach 

set.seed(123)

train_data <- PC2_T2D_cox_df_model1

k <- 10
folds <- createFolds(train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox, k = k, list = TRUE)

# one out-of-sample prediction per training subject
cv_risk_train <- numeric(nrow(train_data))

for (i in seq_len(k)) {
  # fold i is the TEST portion within training
  test_indices_fold  <- folds[[i]]
  train_indices_fold <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(train_data)), test_indices_fold)
  
  train_fold <- train_data[train_indices_fold, ]
  test_fold  <- train_data[test_indices_fold, ]
  
  # Fit Cox model on this fold's training data
  sfit <- coxph(
    Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ rt_PC2*Sex+Age+Total_BMD+BC_PC1+BC_PC2+BC_PC3+BC_PC4+BC_PC5+TTFFMVi,
    data = train_fold
  )
  
  # Predict risk on the held-out fold
  cv_risk_train[test_indices_fold] <- predict(
    sfit,
    newdata = test_fold,
    type = "lp"
  )
}

# Cross-validated ROC/AUC
roc_cv_train_PC2_model_3 <- roc(
  response  = train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox,
  predictor = cv_risk_train,
  quiet     = TRUE
)

auc_cv_train <- auc(roc_cv_train_PC2_model_3)
auc_cv_train
ci_cv_train  <- ci.auc(roc_cv_train_PC2_model_3)
ci_cv_train

#Model 3 (imaging-incident) = 0.7718 (0.7516-0.792)
#Model 3 (admission-incident) = 0.7971 (0.7859, 0.8083)

#Out-of-sample C-index and calibration plots

# Add cross-validated linear predictor to data
train_data$cv_lp <- cv_risk_train

# Compute Harrell's C-index/statistic (chrome-extension://efaidnbmnnnibpcajpcglclefindmkaj/https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/survival/vignettes/concordance.pdf) using cv_lp as the predictor
c_index_cv <- survConcordance(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ cv_lp,
  data = train_data
)

# C-index
c_index_cv$concordance
# 95% CI (approx)
c_index_cv$concordance + c(-1, 1) * 1.96 * c_index_cv$std.err

#C-index (admission-incident) = 0.7926499 (0.7774566, 0.8078432)
#C-index (imaging-incident) = 0.7815625 (0.7530802 0.8100447)

#Calibration plot
library(survival)
library(rms)

# Check outcome is numeric 0/1
train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox_Score <- ifelse(train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox == 2, 1, 0) #re-code to 1 and 0

# Fit the Cox model with the cross-validated linear predictor
sfit <- coxph(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox_Score) ~ cv_lp,
  data = train_data,
  x = TRUE, y = TRUE
)

# Choose a time horizon for calibration (e.g. 90th percentile of follow-up)
time_horizon = 2970 #The day after the last T2D admission incidence: 5601 for admission, 2970 for imaging

# Predicted survival at time_horizon
sf <- survfit(sfit, newdata = train_data)

# Extract S(t) for each subject at time_horizon
# Build a matrix of survival probs: rows = times, cols = subjects
S_mat <- summary(sf, times = time_horizon)$surv
# For a `coxph` with individual newdata, `summary(sf, times=...)$surv` is in subject order
# Predicted risk = 1 - survival
p_hat <- 1 - S_mat   # vector of predicted event probabilities at time_horizon

train_data$pred_risk_t = p_hat

# Observed outcome (0/1)
y <- train_data$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox_Score

#Use 'val.prob.ci.2' function from: https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/CalibrationCurves/versions/2.0.7/topics/val.prob.ci.2
library(CalibrationCurves)
val.prob.ci.2(p_hat, y, CL.smooth="fill", smooth='rcs', nr.knots = 5, col.smooth = 'darkred', lwd.smooth = 2, logistic.cal=TRUE, col.log = 'steelblue', lwd.log = 2, dostats = FALSE, cl.level = FALSE, roundstats = 3, xlab = "Predicted probability", ylab = "Observed probability", pl=TRUE, legendloc = c(.2, .9))

#predictABLE plot for grouped calibration plot

# Voluntary: add predicted risk to train_data df
S_vec <- if (is.matrix(S_mat)) as.numeric(S_mat) else as.numeric(S_mat)
p_hat = 1-S_vec
train_data$pred_risk_t <- S_vec
train_data$pred_risk_t = 1-train_data$pred_risk_t

#Make sure it is in data.frame format - dumb ting
train_data = as.data.frame(train_data)

plotCalibration(data = train_data, cOutcome = 15, predRisk = p_hat, groups = 1000, xlabel = "Predicted probability", ylabel = "Observed probability")

#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
#Plot ROC curves for PC2 and Spine BMFF 
#-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

library(pROC)

# 1. Fit model and get ROC object (code above) - separately for spine and PC2
roc_test = roc_cv_train_spine_model_3
roc_test = roc_cv_train_PC2_model_3

# 2. Compute 95% CI for sensitivity at each specificity (bootstrap)
set.seed(123)
ci_se <- ci.se(
  roc_test,
  boot.n = 2000,       # number of bootstrap samples: 2000 is default
  conf.level = 0.95,
  progress = "none"
)

roc_df <- data.frame(
  specificity = roc_test$specificities,
  sensitivity = roc_test$sensitivities
)
roc_df$fpr <- 1 - roc_df$specificity  # false positive rate

roc_df_Spine = roc_df

roc_df_PC2 = roc_df

# CI band
ci_cols <- colnames(ci_se)            # "2.5%", "50%", "97.5%"
ci_df <- data.frame(
  specificity = as.numeric(rownames(ci_se)),
  se_lower    = ci_se[, ci_cols[1]],
  se_mid      = ci_se[, ci_cols[2]],
  se_upper    = ci_se[, ci_cols[3]]
)
ci_df$fpr <- 1 - ci_df$specificity

ci_df_Spine = ci_df

ci_df_PC2 = ci_df

ggplot() +
  # PC2 ribbon curve
  geom_line(
    data = roc_df_PC2,
    aes(x = fpr, y = sensitivity, color = "PC2"),
    size = 0.5
  ) +
  # Spine BMFF curve
  geom_line(
    data = roc_df_Spine,
    aes(x = fpr, y = sensitivity, color = "Spine BMFF"),
    size = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1,
    intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "grey50"
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Specificity",
    y = "Sensitivity",
    color = "Predictor",
    fill  = "Predictor"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Spine BMFF" = "steelblue", "PC2" = "darkorange"), 
                     labels = c("Spine BMFF" = "Spine BMFF\nAUC = 0.783 (0.773, 0.794)", "PC2" = "PC2\nAUC = 0.797 (0.786, 0.808)"))+
  theme(
    axis.title  = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", lineheight = 1),
    legend.key.height = unit(2.5, "lines")
  )
  
```

Calibration plots using predictABLE: 
```{r}
# Required packages
library(survival)
library(PredictABEL)

# Cox model (as given)
sfit <- coxph(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score) ~ cv_lp,
  data = train_data,
  x = TRUE, y = TRUE
)

# Choose a time horizon for calibration (e.g., day 5601)
time_horizon <- 5601

# Predicted survival at time_horizon for each subject
sf <- survfit(sfit, newdata = train_data)
S_t <- summary(sf, times = time_horizon)$surv   # survival at t for each subject
p_hat <- 1 - S_t                                # predicted risk by time_horizon

# Attach predicted risk
S_vec <- if (is.matrix(S_mat)) as.numeric(S_mat) else as.numeric(S_mat)
p_hat = 1-S_vec
train_data$pred_risk_t <- S_vec
train_data$pred_risk_t = 1-train_data$pred_risk_t

# Construct a binary outcome at the fixed time horizon:
# 1 = event occurred by time_horizon
# 0 = no event by time_horizon
# Exclude subjects censored before time_horizon (cannot determine outcome)
train_data$y_t <- with(train_data,
  as.integer(T2D_TOE_admission_censor <= time_horizon & Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score == 1)
)

# Keep only evaluable subjects (not censored before the horizon)
calib_dat <- subset(
  train_data,
  !(Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score == 0 & T2D_TOE_admission_censor < time_horizon)
)

# Plot calibration with PredictABEL
# Note: Some versions of PredictABEL use argument 'cPredRisk'; others accept 'predRisk'.
# The following uses cPredRisk (most common). Adjust if your version uses 'predRisk'.
PredictABEL::plotCalibration(
  data       = train_data,
  cOutcome   = which(names(calib_dat) == "y_t"),
  cPredRisk  = which(names(calib_dat) == "pred_risk_t"),
  groups     = 10,
  plottitle  = paste0("Calibration at ", time_horizon, " days"),
  xlab       = "Predicted risk",
  ylab       = "Observed risk"
)

rangeaxis <- c(0,0.2) 

plotCalibration(data = train_data, cOutcome = 15, predRisk = train_data$pred_risk_t, groups = 100, rangeaxis= rangeaxis)

plotCalibration(data = train_data, cOutcome = 15, predRisk = p_hat, groups = 1000, xlabel = "Predicted probability", ylabel = "Observed probability")

```


Two other methods of generating calibration plots - did not work quite as well
```{r}
#1. riskRegression package - this worked but not very customizable
library(survival)
library(riskRegression)

## 1. Make sure event is numeric 0/1
train_data$Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score <- as.numeric(train_data$Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score)
# If you had 1/2 before and converted:
# train_data$Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score <- ifelse(train_data$Admission_incident_T2D_cox == 2, 1, 0)

## 2. Restrict to complete cases for time, status, predictor
dd <- subset(
  train_data,
  !is.na(T2D_TOE_admission_censor) &
  !is.na(Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score) &
  !is.na(cv_lp)
)

## Quick sanity checks
stopifnot(
  length(unique(dd$Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score)) > 1,       # need events and non-events
  all(dd$Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score %in% c(0, 1)),
  nrow(dd) > 0
)

## 3. Fit Cox model (optional; Score can also fit from formula)
sfit <- coxph(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score) ~ cv_lp,
  data = dd,
  x = TRUE, y = TRUE
)

## 4. Use Score with consistent outcome variable
time_horizon <- 5601  # or any time within the observed follow-up

xs <- Score(
  object  = list(cv_model = sfit),
  formula = Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score) ~ 1,
  data    = dd,
  times   = time_horizon,
  metrics = "Brier",
  plots   = "calibration"
)

plotCalibration(xs, models = "cv_model", legend = TRUE, brier.in.legend = TRUE)

#2. CailbrationCurves: https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/CalibrationCurves/vignettes/CalibrationCurves.html - requires some tweaking
library(CalibrationCurves)
sfit <- coxph(
  Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox_Score) ~ cv_lp,
  data = train_data,
  x = TRUE, y = TRUE
)

calPerf = valProbSurvival(sfit, train_data, plotCal = "ggplot", nk = 5)
```

For-loop the k-fold CV:ed process above:
```{r}
library(survival)
library(caret)
library(pROC)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

set.seed(123)

#----------------------------------
# 1. Exposure datasets and variables
#----------------------------------

exposure_specs <- list(
  Spine = list(
    data = Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df,
    cont = "rt_BMFF",
    bmd  = "Spine_BMD",
    # Tertiles
    tert_imaging_all = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_M   = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_F   = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident",
    tert_adm_all     = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident",
    tert_adm_M       = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident",
    tert_adm_F       = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"
  ),
  PC2 = list(
    data = Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df,
    cont = "rt_PC2",
    bmd  = "Total_BMD",
    tert_imaging_all = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_M   = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_F   = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident",
    tert_adm_all     = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident",
    tert_adm_M       = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident",
    tert_adm_F       = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"
  )
)

#-------------------------------
# 2. Outcomes and Surv() pieces
#-------------------------------

outcome_specs <- list(
  imaging = list(
    surv   = "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)",
    time   = "T2D_TOE_imaging_censor",
    event  = "Imaging_incident_T2D_cox"
  ),
  admission = list(
    surv   = "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)",
    time   = "T2D_TOE_admission_censor",
    event  = "Admission_incident_T2D_cox"
  )
)

sex_groups     <- c("All", "M", "F")
exposure_types <- c("continuous", "tertiles")  # continuous vs tertiles
model_nos      <- 1:3                           # Model 1, 2, 3
k <- 10L

#--------------------------------
# 3. Helper: pick tertile variable
#--------------------------------

get_tert_var <- function(exp_spec, outcome_name, sex_group) {
  if (outcome_name == "imaging") {
    if (sex_group == "All") {
      exp_spec$tert_imaging_all
    } else if (sex_group == "M") {
      exp_spec$tert_imaging_M
    } else {
      exp_spec$tert_imaging_F
    }
  } else { # admission
    if (sex_group == "All") {
      exp_spec$tert_adm_all
    } else if (sex_group == "M") {
      exp_spec$tert_adm_M
    } else {
      exp_spec$tert_adm_F
    }
  }
}

#--------------------------------
# 4. Main CV loop
#--------------------------------

cv_auc_results_list <- list()
res_idx <- 1L

for (exp_name in names(exposure_specs)) {

  exp_spec <- exposure_specs[[exp_name]]
  dat_full <- exp_spec$data

  for (outcome_name in names(outcome_specs)) {

    surv_part <- outcome_specs[[outcome_name]]$surv
    time_var  <- outcome_specs[[outcome_name]]$time
    event_var <- outcome_specs[[outcome_name]]$event

    for (sex_group in sex_groups) {

      for (exposure_type in exposure_types) {

        # Determine exposure variable (continuous or tertile)
        if (exposure_type == "continuous") {
          x_var <- exp_spec$cont
        } else {
          x_var <- get_tert_var(exp_spec, outcome_name, sex_group)
          if (!x_var %in% names(dat_full)) {
            warning(sprintf(
              "Skipping tertile model: exposure=%s, outcome=%s, sex_group=%s (column %s not found)",
              exp_name, outcome_name, sex_group, x_var
            ))
            next
          }
        }

        for (model_no in model_nos) {

          #------------------------
          # 4a. Define covariates by model_no
          #------------------------
          # All models use Age; Sex is only in the interaction when sex_group == "All"
          if (model_no == 1) {
            covars_extra <- c("BMI")
          } else if (model_no == 2) {
            covars_extra <- c("BMI", exp_spec$bmd)
          } else if (model_no == 3) {
            covars_extra <- c(
              exp_spec$bmd,
              "BC_PC1","BC_PC2","BC_PC3","BC_PC4","BC_PC5","TTFFMVi"
            )
          }

          #------------------------
          # 4b. Build dataset
          #------------------------
          dat <- dat_full

          if (sex_group != "All") {
            dat <- dat %>% dplyr::filter(Sex == sex_group)
          }

          needed_vars <- unique(c(
            "Sex", "Age", x_var, time_var, event_var, covars_extra
          ))

          dat <- dat %>%
            dplyr::select(dplyr::all_of(needed_vars)) %>%
            tidyr::drop_na()

          # Skip if insufficient data or no variation in event
          if (nrow(dat) == 0L || length(unique(dat[[event_var]])) < 2L) {
            next
          }

          #------------------------
          # 4c. Build Cox formula
          #------------------------
          if (sex_group == "All") {
            # Interaction with Sex
            rhs_exp <- sprintf("%s*Sex", x_var)
          } else {
            # No Sex in sex-stratified models
            rhs_exp <- x_var
          }

          rhs_terms <- c(rhs_exp, "Age", covars_extra[ covars_extra != "Age" ])
          rhs <- paste(rhs_terms, collapse = " + ")
          form_str <- sprintf("%s ~ %s", surv_part, rhs)

          #------------------------
          # 4d. Create folds
          #------------------------
          k_use <- min(k, nrow(dat))
          folds <- createFolds(dat[[event_var]], k = k_use, list = TRUE)

          cv_risk <- numeric(nrow(dat))

          #------------------------
          # 4e. k-fold CV
          #------------------------
          for (i in seq_along(folds)) {

            test_idx  <- folds[[i]]
            train_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(nrow(dat)), test_idx)

            train_fold <- dat[train_idx, , drop = FALSE]
            test_fold  <- dat[test_idx,  , drop = FALSE]

            sfit <- coxph(
              as.formula(form_str),
              data = train_fold
            )

            cv_risk[test_idx] <- predict(
              sfit,
              newdata = test_fold,
              type = "lp"
            )
          }

          #------------------------
          # 4f. ROC / AUC + 95% CI
          #------------------------
          roc_obj <- roc(
            response  = dat[[event_var]],
            predictor = cv_risk,
            quiet     = TRUE
          )

          auc_val <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
          ci_vec  <- as.numeric(ci.auc(roc_obj))  # c(lower, AUC, upper)

          auc_lcl <- ci_vec
          auc_ucl <- ci_vec

          #------------------------
          # 4g. Store ONE ROW per model
          #------------------------
          cv_auc_results_list[[res_idx]] <- data.frame(
            exposure      = exp_name,          # "Spine" or "PC2"
            outcome       = outcome_name,      # "imaging" or "admission"
            sex_group     = sex_group,         # "All", "M", "F"
            exposure_type = exposure_type,     # "continuous" or "tertiles"
            model_no      = model_no,          # 1, 2, 3
            predictor_var = x_var,             # rt_BMFF / rt_PC2 / tertile name
            auc           = auc_val,
            auc_lcl       = auc_lcl,
            auc_ucl       = auc_ucl,
            stringsAsFactors = FALSE
          )

          res_idx <- res_idx + 1L
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

cv_auc_spine_pc2 <- bind_rows(cv_auc_results_list)

processed_list <- lapply(cv_auc_results_list, function(df_i) {
  # Require at least 3 rows and 6 columns
  if (nrow(df_i) < 3 || ncol(df_i) < 6) return(NULL)

  # Take row 1, columns 1:8
  out <- df_i[1, 1:8, drop = FALSE]

  # Add auc_ucl from row 3, column 9
  out$auc_ucl <- df_i[3, 9]

  out
})

# Remove any NULLs and bind into final data frame
final_cv_auc_spine_pc2_results <- bind_rows(processed_list)

```



Run all of the Cox regressions above with WHR as a clinical comparator 
```{r}
#Treat WHR as spine BMFF and PC2 - use the complete-case analysis approach, i.e., obtain WHR measurements available for all participants in the imaging cohort without imputing missing values

#WHR_imaging_df: contains the WHR measurements from the first and second imaging visits (no repeat-measurements) - merge this with the Imaging_date df to obtain the WHR available for the MRI cohort:
WHR_MRI_date_df = merge(WHR_imaging_df, Imaging_date, by='eid')
WHR_MRI_date_df = merge(WHR_MRI_date_df, Age_sex_imaging_df, by='eid')
count(is.na(WHR_MRI_date_df$WHR_first_second_imaging)) #no missing data for any variables

#Run Cox regressions with sex and age as covariates (maybe BMD and TTFFMVi - depending on model performance)- do not include adiposity PCs as this might introduce collider bias and multicollinearity

#Imaging-incident - merge with updated 'T2D_df' to get the prevelant cases as 'NA' NOT FALSE! - Also remove any imaging date NAs - 2 found
WHR_imaging_incident_df = merge(WHR_MRI_date_df, T2D_df[,c(1,3,5:8)], by='eid')
WHR_imaging_incident_df =  WHR_imaging_incident_df %>% filter(!is.na(Imaging_date)) #no missing dates

#Censor date
max(na.omit(WHR_imaging_incident_df$Date_imaging_incident_T2D)) #2022-10-27
max(na.omit(WHR_imaging_incident_df$Date_admission_incident_T2D)) #2022-10-27
WHR_imaging_incident_df = WHR_imaging_incident_df %>% mutate(max_follow_up = as.Date('2022-10-28'))

#Get the admission dates for all participants
WHR_imaging_incident_df = merge(WHR_imaging_incident_df, ukb35190_date_assessment_centre[,c(1,2)], by='eid')
colnames(WHR_imaging_incident_df)[12] = 'Admission_date'

#Max follow-up time

#Imaging
WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_time_imaging_incident = WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_follow_up - WHR_imaging_incident_df$Imaging_date 
WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_time_imaging_incident = as.numeric(WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_time_imaging_incident)

#Admission
WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_time_admission_incident = WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_follow_up - WHR_imaging_incident_df$Admission_date 
WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_time_admission_incident = as.numeric(WHR_imaging_incident_df$max_time_admission_incident)


#T2D incidence time 

#Imaging
WHR_imaging_incident_df$T2D_time_to_event_imaging = WHR_imaging_incident_df$Date_imaging_incident_T2D-WHR_imaging_incident_df$Imaging_date
WHR_imaging_incident_df$T2D_time_to_event_imaging = as.numeric(WHR_imaging_incident_df$T2D_time_to_event_imaging)

#Replace max-time with time to T2D incidence for cases
WHR_imaging_incident_df <- WHR_imaging_incident_df %>%
  mutate(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor = ifelse(!is.na(T2D_time_to_event_imaging),
                                     T2D_time_to_event_imaging,
                                     max_time_imaging_incident))

WHR_imaging_incident_df$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox <- ifelse(WHR_imaging_incident_df$Incident_T2D, 2, 1)

#Admission
WHR_imaging_incident_df$T2D_time_to_event_admission = WHR_imaging_incident_df$Date_admission_incident_T2D-WHR_imaging_incident_df$Admission_date
WHR_imaging_incident_df$T2D_time_to_event_admission = as.numeric(WHR_imaging_incident_df$T2D_time_to_event_admission)

#Replace max-time with time to T2D incidence for cases
WHR_imaging_incident_df <- WHR_imaging_incident_df %>%
  mutate(T2D_TOE_admission_censor = ifelse(!is.na(T2D_time_to_event_admission),
                                     T2D_time_to_event_admission,
                                     max_time_admission_incident))

#!! re-code any accidental admission incident T2D cases that are prevalent
WHR_imaging_incident_df <- WHR_imaging_incident_df %>%
  mutate(
    T2D_time_to_event_admission = as.numeric(T2D_time_to_event_admission),
    Incident_admission_T2D = ifelse(
      !is.na(T2D_time_to_event_admission) & T2D_time_to_event_admission < 180,
      NA,  # set to NA when time-to-event < 180
      Incident_admission_T2D
    )
  )

WHR_imaging_incident_df$Admission_incident_T2D_cox <- ifelse(WHR_imaging_incident_df$Incident_admission_T2D, 2, 1)

#Last check to ensure that imaging and admission incident T2D are >6 months after imaging/admission 
min_time_true <- WHR_imaging_incident_df %>%
  filter(Incident_T2D == TRUE) %>%
  pull(T2D_time_to_event_imaging) %>%
  min(na.rm = TRUE)

min_time_true #183 and 182 for imaging-incident and admission-incident, respectively.

WHR_imaging_incident_df$rt_WHR = RankNorm(WHR_imaging_incident_df$WHR_first_second_imaging)
colnames(WHR_imaging_incident_df)[4] = 'Age'

#Optimal tertile stratification - re-run for PC2 and spine BMFF as well without BMI adjustment 

#WHR
adapted_WHR_imaging_incident_df <-
  WHR_imaging_incident_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Admission_incident_T2D_cox))

adapted_WHR_imaging_incident_df <- WHR_imaging_incident_df %>%
  filter(Sex == "M", !is.na(Admission_incident_T2D_cox))

# Define the function to calculate AUC for given thresholds
calculate_auc <- function(thresholds) {
  tertiles <- cut(
    adapted_WHR_imaging_incident_df$rt_WHR,
    breaks = c(-Inf, thresholds, Inf),
    include.lowest = TRUE
  )
  # Fit the Cox model
  mv_fit <- coxph(
    Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox) ~ tertiles + Age,
    data = adapted_WHR_imaging_incident_df
  )
  # Calculate the linear predictor
  lp <- predict(mv_fit, type = "lp")
  # Get ROC curve and AUC
  roc_curve <- roc(adapted_WHR_imaging_incident_df$Admission_incident_T2D_cox, lp)
  auc(roc_curve)
}

# Select potential thresholds based on quantiles: use either 0.01/0.1 and 0.9/0.99 by=0.1 for slightly different thresholds but similar AUC --> I stuck to 0.1 and 0.9 since it took a bit less time to compute 
possible_thresholds <- quantile(adapted_WHR_imaging_incident_df$rt_WHR, probs = seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1))

best_auc <- 0
best_thresholds <- NULL

# Iterate over threshold combinations
for (t1 in possible_thresholds) {
  for (t2 in possible_thresholds) {
    if (t1 < t2) {
      current_auc <- calculate_auc(c(t1, t2))
      if (current_auc > best_auc) {
        best_auc <- current_auc
        best_thresholds <- c(t1, t2)
      }
    }
  }
}

cat("Best AUC:", best_auc, "with thresholds:", best_thresholds, "\n")

#Spine BMFF
adapted_Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df <-
  Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Imaging_incident_T2D_cox))

# Define the function to calculate AUC for given thresholds
calculate_auc <- function(thresholds) {
  tertiles <- cut(
    adapted_Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df$rt_BMFF,
    breaks = c(-Inf, thresholds, Inf),
    include.lowest = TRUE
  )
  # Fit the Cox model
  mv_fit <- coxph(
    Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ tertiles * Sex + Age,
    data = adapted_Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df
  )
  # Calculate the linear predictor
  lp <- predict(mv_fit, type = "lp")
  # Get ROC curve and AUC
  roc_curve <- roc(adapted_Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox, lp)
  auc(roc_curve)
}

# Select potential thresholds based on quantiles: use either 0.01/0.1 and 0.9/0.99 by=0.1 for slightly different thresholds but similar AUC --> I stuck to 0.1 and 0.9 since it took a bit less time to compute 
possible_thresholds <- quantile(adapted_Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df$rt_BMFF, probs = seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1))

best_auc <- 0
best_thresholds <- NULL

# Iterate over threshold combinations
for (t1 in possible_thresholds) {
  for (t2 in possible_thresholds) {
    if (t1 < t2) {
      current_auc <- calculate_auc(c(t1, t2))
      if (current_auc > best_auc) {
        best_auc <- current_auc
        best_thresholds <- c(t1, t2)
      }
    }
  }
}

cat("Best AUC:", best_auc, "with thresholds:", best_thresholds, "\n")

#PC2
adapted_Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df <-
  Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Imaging_incident_T2D_cox))

# Define the function to calculate AUC for given thresholds
calculate_auc <- function(thresholds) {
  tertiles <- cut(
    adapted_Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df$rt_PC2,
    breaks = c(-Inf, thresholds, Inf),
    include.lowest = TRUE
  )
  # Fit the Cox model
  mv_fit <- coxph(
    Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox) ~ tertiles * Sex + Age,
    data = adapted_Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df
  )
  # Calculate the linear predictor
  lp <- predict(mv_fit, type = "lp")
  # Get ROC curve and AUC
  roc_curve <- roc(adapted_Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df$Imaging_incident_T2D_cox, lp)
  auc(roc_curve)
}

# Select potential thresholds based on quantiles: use either 0.01/0.1 and 0.9/0.99 by=0.1 for slightly different thresholds but similar AUC --> I stuck to 0.1 and 0.9 since it took a bit less time to compute 
possible_thresholds <- quantile(adapted_Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df$rt_PC2, probs = seq(0.1, 0.9, by = 0.1))

best_auc <- 0
best_thresholds <- NULL

# Iterate over threshold combinations
for (t1 in possible_thresholds) {
  for (t2 in possible_thresholds) {
    if (t1 < t2) {
      current_auc <- calculate_auc(c(t1, t2))
      if (current_auc > best_auc) {
        best_auc <- current_auc
        best_thresholds <- c(t1, t2)
      }
    }
  }
}

cat("Best AUC:", best_auc, "with thresholds:", best_thresholds, "\n")

#Determine the best AUC for the thresholds 

#WHR - both
#WHR admission incident: AUC=0.7093533 with thresholds: -0.5367556, 0.8245012 
#WHR imaging incident: AUC=0.6815727 with thresholds: -0.02864989, 0.7982314 

#WHR - males
#WHR admission incident: AUC=0.6771906 with thresholds: 0.4095065 1.245568
#WHR imaging incident: AUC=0.6362573 with thresholds: 0.7574628 1.217597 

#WHR - females
#WHR admission incident: AUC=0.7330869 with thresholds: -0.6178768 0.3438335 
#WHR imaging incident: AUC=0.6951578 with thresholds: -0.8148092 -0.02075808 

#Create columns with tertile thresholds

#Both sexes

cut_offs <- c(-Inf, 0.4095065, 1.245568, Inf)

# Use the cut function to create tertiles
WHR_imaging_incident_df$WHR_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident <- cut(
  WHR_imaging_incident_df$rt_WHR,
  breaks = cut_offs,
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = c("T1", "T2", "T3")
)

cut_offs <- c(-Inf, -0.6178768, 0.3438335, Inf)

#Sex-stratified

# Ensure column is a factor with desired levels
WHR_imaging_incident_df$WHR_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident <- factor(NA, levels = c("T1", "T2", "T3"))

# Logical index for females
idx_female <- WHR_imaging_incident_df$Sex == "F"

# Assign tertiles for females
WHR_imaging_incident_df$WHR_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident[idx_female] <- cut(
  WHR_imaging_incident_df$rt_WHR[idx_female],
  breaks = cut_offs,
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  labels = c("T1", "T2", "T3")
)

export(WHR_imaging_incident_df, 'MV_Cox_WHR_df.csv')

#Spine BMFF admission incident: AUC=0.6545816 with thresholds: 0.244955, 1.271262 
#Spine BMFF incident: AUC=0.6297202 with thresholds: -0.5392428, 1.257695

#PC2 admission incident: AUC=0.7016217 with thresholds: -0.0106823, 1.262787 
#PC2 imaging incident: AUC=0.6710972 with thresholds: -0.8610551, 0.4976318  - differs from the model 1 (including BMI) adjusted model tertile cut-offs, hence only add columns for this


#Use the 'no_BMI' PC2 and Spine BMFF tertiles where the thresholds differ based on the removal of BMI adjustment for WHR comparison

#Run tertile Cox/survival regressions comparing the effect estimates and predictive performances of each exposure in the same adjusted models (no BMI adjustment) 

#Imaging incident analyses shown below: replace the T2D_TOE_imaging_censor with T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox with Admission_incident_T2D_cox, Incident_T2D with Incident_admission_T2D and PC2_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident with PC2_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident when running admission incident analyses - run both and store results for imaging incident and admission incident analyses separately in the the results data frame
KM_curve_imaging_incident_df = Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df %>% filter(!is.na(Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df$Incident_T2D))
km_fit <- survfit(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~PC2_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident, data = KM_curve_imaging_incident_df)
km_fit 

#Normalised exposure: replace the T2D_TOE_imaging_censor with T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox with Admission_incident_T2D_cox for admission incident analyses
mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_PC2*Sex+Age, data=Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df)
mv_fit
summary(mv_fit) 

#Tertiles of exposure: replace the T2D_TOE_imaging_censor with T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox with Admission_incident_T2D_cox for admission incident analyses
mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~PC2_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident*Sex+Age, data=Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df)
mv_fit 
summary(mv_fit) 

#Sex-stratified imaging incident analyses: replace the T2D_TOE_imaging_censor with T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox with Admission_incident_T2D_cox, Incident_T2D with Incident_admission_T2D and PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident with PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident when running admission incident analyses and replace the 'M' with 'F' for the female analyses - run both for each sex separately and store results for imaging incident and admission incident analyses separately in the the results data frame, and separately for males (M) and females (F)
KM_curve_imaging_incident_df = Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df %>% filter(!is.na(Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df$Incident_T2D))
km_fit <- survfit(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident, data = KM_curve_imaging_incident_df)
km_fit

#Normalised exposure: replace Sex='M' with Sex='F' for female analyses and replace the T2D_TOE_imaging_censor with T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox with Admission_incident_T2D_cox for admission incident analyses; do both for each sex separately and store separately in the results data frame
mv_fit <- subset(Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df, Sex=='M') %>% coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~rt_PC2+Age, data=.)
mv_fit 
summary(mv_fit) 

#Tertiles of exposure: replace the T2D_TOE_imaging_censor with T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox with Admission_incident_T2D_cox and PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident with PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident when running admission incident analyses and replace the 'M' with 'F' for the female analyses for admission incident analyses; do both for each sex separately and store separately in the results data frame
mv_fit <- coxph(Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)~PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident+Age, data=Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df)
mv_fit 
summary(mv_fit) 

```


For-loop to generate summary statistics for the WHR vs spine BMFF and PC2 regressions - run the tertile regressions where the optimal thresholds differ due to no-BMI adjustment manually
```{r}
library(survival)
library(dplyr)

#---------------------------
# Helper: extract Cox results
#---------------------------
extract_cox_results <- function(fit, model_id, term_filter = NULL) {
  s  <- summary(fit)
  df <- as.data.frame(s$coefficients)
  ci <- as.data.frame(s$conf.int)

  out <- df %>%
    mutate(
      term = rownames(df),
      HR   = ci$`exp(coef)`,
      LCL  = ci$`lower .95`,
      UCL  = ci$`upper .95`
    ) %>%
    select(term, HR, LCL, UCL, p = `Pr(>|z|)`)

  if (!is.null(term_filter)) {
    out <- out %>% filter(grepl(term_filter, term))
  }

  out %>%
    mutate(model_id = model_id, .before = 1)
}

#===========================
# COMMON SETTINGS STRUCTURE
#===========================

# For each exposure define:
#   - label: "PC2", "Spine", "WHR"
#   - data:  corresponding data frame
#   - cont_var: continuous exposure variable
#   - tertile prefixes (PC2_*, Spine_*, WHR_*)

exposure_specs <- list(
  PC2 = list(
    label   = "PC2",
    df      = Model_adjusted_PC2_imaging_incident_df,
    cont    = "rt_PC2",
    tert_imaging_overall   = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_no_BMI_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_M         = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_no_BMI_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_F         = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident",
    tert_admission_overall = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident",
    tert_admission_M       = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_M_no_BMI_admission_incident",
    tert_admission_F       = "PC2_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"
  ),
  Spine = list(
    label   = "Spine",
    df      = Model_adjusted_Spine_imaging_incident_df,
    cont    = "rt_BMFF",
    tert_imaging_overall   = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_M         = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_F         = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_no_BMI_imaging_incident",
    tert_admission_overall = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident",
    tert_admission_M       = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident",
    tert_admission_F       = "Spine_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"
  ),
  WHR = list(
    label   = "WHR",
    df      = WHR_imaging_incident_df,
    cont    = "rt_WHR",
    tert_imaging_overall   = "WHR_optimal_tertiles_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_M         = "WHR_optimal_tertiles_M_imaging_incident",
    tert_imaging_F         = "WHR_optimal_tertiles_F_imaging_incident",
    tert_admission_overall = "WHR_optimal_tertiles_admission_incident",
    tert_admission_M       = "WHR_optimal_tertiles_M_admission_incident",
    tert_admission_F       = "WHR_optimal_tertiles_F_admission_incident"
  )
)

#==========================================================
# 1. OVERALL imaging vs admission (for all three exposures)
#==========================================================

settings_overall <- tibble::tribble(
  ~analysis,   ~cont_surv_imaging,                                               ~cont_surv_admission,
  "imaging",   "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)",         "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)",
  "admission", "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)",         "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)"
)

res_overall_all_exposures <- list()
idx <- 1L

for (exp_name in names(exposure_specs)) {

  exp <- exposure_specs[[exp_name]]

  for (analysis in c("imaging", "admission")) {

    dat      <- exp$df
    cont_x   <- exp$cont

    if (analysis == "imaging") {
      surv_part   <- "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)"
      tert_overall <- exp$tert_imaging_overall
    } else {
      surv_part   <- "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)"
      tert_overall <- exp$tert_admission_overall
    }

    #------------------#
    # Continuous model #
    #------------------#
    cont_formula <- as.formula(
      sprintf("%s ~ %s*Sex + Age", surv_part, cont_x)
    )

    mv_fit_cont <- coxph(cont_formula, data = dat)

    res_overall_all_exposures[[idx]] <-
      extract_cox_results(
        mv_fit_cont,
        model_id    = paste0(exp$label, "_", analysis, "_overall_continuous"),
        term_filter = cont_x
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        exposure = exp$label,
        analysis = analysis,
        type     = "overall_continuous",
        .before  = 1
      )
    idx <- idx + 1L

    #----------------#
    # Tertiles model #
    #----------------#
    tert_formula <- as.formula(
      sprintf("%s ~ %s*Sex + Age", surv_part, tert_overall)
    )

    mv_fit_tertiles <- coxph(tert_formula, data = dat)

    res_overall_all_exposures[[idx]] <-
      extract_cox_results(
        mv_fit_tertiles,
        model_id    = paste0(exp$label, "_", analysis, "_overall_tertiles"),
        term_filter = tert_overall
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        exposure = exp$label,
        analysis = analysis,
        type     = "overall_tertiles",
        .before  = 1
      )
    idx <- idx + 1L
  }
}

res_overall_all_exposures <- bind_rows(res_overall_all_exposures)

#==========================================================
# 2. Sex-stratified imaging vs admission (continuous)
#==========================================================

settings_sex <- expand.grid(
  analysis = c("imaging", "admission"),
  sex      = c("M", "F"),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

res_sex_cont_all_exposures <- list()
idx <- 1L

for (exp_name in names(exposure_specs)) {

  exp <- exposure_specs[[exp_name]]

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(settings_sex))) {
    a   <- settings_sex[i, ]
    dat <- exp$df %>% filter(Sex == a$sex)
    cont_x <- exp$cont

    if (a$analysis == "imaging") {
      surv_part <- "Surv(T2D_TOE_imaging_censor, Imaging_incident_T2D_cox)"
    } else {
      surv_part <- "Surv(T2D_TOE_admission_censor, Admission_incident_T2D_cox)"
    }

    cont_formula <- as.formula(
      sprintf("%s ~ %s + Age", surv_part, cont_x)
    )

    mv_fit_sex <- coxph(cont_formula, data = dat)

    res_sex_cont_all_exposures[[idx]] <-
      extract_cox_results(
        mv_fit_sex,
        model_id    = paste0(exp$label, "_", a$analysis, "_", a$sex, "_continuous"),
        term_filter = cont_x
      ) %>%
      mutate(
        exposure = exp$label,
        analysis = a$analysis,
        sex      = a$sex,
        type     = "sex_continuous",
        .before  = 1
      )
    idx <- idx + 1L
  }
}

res_sex_cont_all_exposures <- bind_rows(res_sex_cont_all_exposures)

#==========================================================
# 3. Sex-stratified imaging vs admission (tertiles)
#==========================================================

res_sex_tertiles_all_exposures <- list()
idx <- 1L

for (exp_name in names(exposure_specs)) {

  exp <- exposure_specs[[exp_name]]

  for (i in seq_len(nrow(settings_sex))) {
    a   <- settings_sex[i, ]
    dat <- exp$df %>%
      dplyr::filter(Sex == a$sex) %>%
      droplevels()
```
